source_jobs.title: Scientific & Regulatory Product Specialist
source_jobs.description:
MISSION

Ensure Uncountable’s regulatory, environmental, and chemistry data provides a trusted baseline for intelligence across the platform, by shaping how third-party ingredient data is sourced, interpreted, and productized.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Work with Uncountable's engineering team to synthesize data from external regulatory, environmental, and chemistry data providers into actionable information for its end product

 - Evaluate, onboard, and manage ingredient and regulatory databases

 - Translate regulatory requirements into clear data models, rules, and customer-facing workflows

 - Ensure data accuracy, traceability, and regulatory relevance over time

 - Serve as the internal expert on regulatory data across Product, Engineering, and Customer teams


REQUIRED BACKGROUND

 - Regulatory affairs experience in chemicals, food, cosmetics, materials, or life sciences

 - Hands-on experience authoring Safety Data Sheets (SDSs) in the US and/or EU

 - Familiarity with ingredient and regulatory databases

 - Strong ability to operationalize regulatory knowledge within software products


WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

This role ensures Uncountable’s intelligence layer is built on credible, defensible regulatory data, enabling customers to make compliant and informed decisions with confidence. Providing scientists with critical, up-to-date context helps them develop new products faster.

Location:

 - This role can be either remote in one of our 4 office locations (SF, NY, London, Munich)

source_jobs.title: Junior Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
Description

We’re looking for an ambitious go-getter to join our sales and business development organization. With an enormous pool of potential customers growing everyday as new startups arise, Uncountable is looking to bring on a new account executive to keep up with inbound interest and more heavily pursue warm opportunities. You will have the opportunity to work cross-functionally and be instrumental to shaping the product and the team.

 

Key responsibilities

Experience developing and maintaining outbound campaigns over email, phone, LinkedIn and other techniques

Delivering sales pitches to prospective customers both over the phone and in-person

Providing demos of Uncountable's web platform

Building proposals and handling negotiations

Coordinating sales efforts to ensure Uncountable is always putting our best foot forward

 

Requirements

Professional experience in software (or similar) sales

Strong individual contributor with great collaboration skills

Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

Positive attitude and willingness to learn; someone who is excited to roll up his or her sleeves and dive in

Availability to travel ~15% of the time

Availability to take morning calls with the east coast and Europe

 

Preferred Qualifications

Past experience working in an early stage start-up environment

Past experience selling a technical product

Familiarity with large enterprise sales techniques

Knowledge of chemistry/materials science and statistics

Passion for technology, chemistry, or material science



Salary: 70-90K Base, 140-200K OTE + Equity

source_jobs.title: Head of Demand Generation
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in Uncountable!




DESCRIPTION

Uncountable is seeking an experienced marketing operator who is passionate about scaling demand generation and creating outsized leverage through modern marketing practices. Uncountable’s goal is to revolutionize industrial research and development. Help us build a best-in-class go to market motion to scale the company and our impact.

This is an individual contributor role that reports directly to our CEO and is an opportunity to help build out the marketing team and a fast-growing technology company.



Salary Range: $100K - $140K + Equity

Location: New York or London (Hybrid)






RESPONSIBILITIES

You will have full autonomy to build a scalable, data-driven customer acquisition machine from the ground up.



 - Full-Cycle Campaign Ownership: Design and execute the entire lead lifecycle—from initial ad creative and SEM strategy to automated nurturing sequences and sales handoff.

 - Budget & ROI Accountability: Allocate spend across LinkedIn, Google Ads, Bing and industry publications

 - SEO & AEO: Work to improve our websites positioning in search and answer engine rankings.

 - Account-Based Marketing (ABM): Develop and execute account strategies to penetrate enterprise R&D organizations in chemicals, materials science, and CPG.

 - Data Analysis & Attribution: Build the dashboards that track pipeline velocity, conversion rates, and multi-touch attribution.






PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

We hire for ability over experience. However, familiarity with best practices in enterprise SaaS companies is necessary for success.



 - SaaS Experience: B2B Enterprise SaaS marketing, specifically in demand gen or growth roles. Experience in an early-stage environment is highly preferred.

 - Technical or Scientific Fluency: Ability to speak the language of R&D directors and IT stakeholders. You must be unafraid to explain why a centralized data schema matters to a scientist.






BENEFITS

 - Competitive Salary and Equity

 - Health and Dental Insurance

 - 401K with Employer Contribution

source_jobs.title: Product Manager (New Grad)
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in Uncountable Engineering!

 

Description

Uncountable is looking for a product manager who recently graduated from or will graduate from university. This role is perfect for a high-achieving CS student who is interested in breaking into product management right away or looking to gain more well-rounded product and business experience before starting their own company. This will be a fast paced role with a lot of responsibility and a steep learning curve.

‍

Our goal is to revolutionize industrial research and development.  We're looking for a motivated PM who can help to build a state of the art development platform that will be used across Fortune 500 companies.

 

This role is an in-person role working out of either our San Francisco or New York office.

 

Primary Responsibility

You primary responsibility will be to prioritize features, plan projects, triage issues, and assist engineers.

 

Examples:

- Writing up 20+ tickets on a weekly basis with implementation plans

- Developing planning documents for larger projects

- Making judgement calls about which customer-facing issues to work on next

- Participating in management-level discussions and company strategic initiatives to get a broader perspective on how the Uncountable platform should evolve

 
 

Important Caveats

Read these before applying.

- Uncountable is an engineering-first organization and needs a PM that can work closely with engineers. We will not consider applications from those without a CS degree. You will be asked to write code during the interview process.

- Uncountable values execution over idea generation. The leverage you will have as a PM will be a function of the hours you put in writing tickets, assisting engineers, and more. This is a role where expect a lot of hard work.

 

Salary: $120-135K + equity

 

Requirements

- Computer science bachelor's degree

- 2+ internship experiences in software development

- Demonstrated interest in product or entrepreneurship

‍‍

Our Current Stack

Backend: Flask (Python), Postgres

Frontend: React (Typescript), Redux, Sass

 

Benefits

- Competitive Salary and Equity

- Health and Dental Insurance

- 401K with Employer Contribution



What's next? Learn more about Interviewing at Uncountable https://www.uncountable.com/interviewing-at-uncountable

 

Learn more about our engineering team: Check out our blog https://medium.com/uncountable-engineering

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Implementations
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in Uncountable Implementations Engineering!




ABOUT UNCOUNTABLE

Science moves slowly — not because researchers aren't brilliant, but because their tools haven't kept up. Uncountable is changing that. We build a unified R&D platform used by the world's leading chemists, material scientists, and biologists to dramatically accelerate how new products are discovered, developed, and brought to market.

Founded by engineers from MIT and Stanford, and trusted by enterprise R&D organizations across chemicals, pharmaceuticals, advanced materials, and food & beverage, Uncountable is on a mission to accelerate industrial R&D by an order of magnitude. Implementations Engineering is a small, high-velocity team — and every person here makes a real difference.




THE ROLE

We're looking for a scientifically-minded software engineer to join our Implementations Engineering team. This is a rare hybrid role that sits at the intersection of software development and scientific domain expertise, where you'll spend your time building real, production-grade software that connects Uncountable's platform to the actual instruments, systems, and workflows used in R&D labs around the world.

When a biotechnology company wants their HPLC instrument to automatically push results into Uncountable, or a materials company needs their legacy LIMS data migrated and mapped into a structured format, you're the engineer who makes that happen. You'll write Python scripts, design data pipelines, connect to APIs, and work directly with customers to understand their data environments and deliver solutions that save their scientists hours every week.

This isn't a support role — it's an engineering role with a domain twist. You'll have real ownership over your work, interact directly with customers, and see the direct impact of what you build.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build data integrations that ingest outputs from lab instruments (spectrometers, rheometers, HPLCs, and more) into Uncountable's platform

 - Design and implement automated data transfer pipelines that connect customer systems — ELNs, LIMS, spreadsheets, databases — to Uncountable

 - Write Python scripts and tooling to parse, validate, and transform scientific data files in a wide variety of formats

 - Work directly with customer technical teams to understand their data environments, map their schemas, and deliver clean, well-documented integrations

 - Collaborate with Uncountable's core engineering team to identify integration patterns that can be built into the product itself

 - Debug and maintain existing integrations as customer needs evolve and data sources change

 - Contribute to internal tooling and shared libraries that make future integrations faster and more reliable




REQUIREMENTS

 - Programming experience: Coursework or professional experience equivalent to 4 university-level CS courses; comfort writing Python or another scripting language to process data files

 - Scientific background: Meaningful knowledge of chemistry, materials science, biology, or a related life/physical science — enough to understand what a researcher means when they talk about their data

 - Genuine curiosity about software engineering and how technology can transform scientific workflows

 - Problem-solving mindset: Comfortable diving into unfamiliar file formats, APIs, and data structures to figure things out




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - B.S. in Computer Science, Chemical Engineering, Materials Science, Chemistry, Bioengineering, or a related field

 - Experience working with APIs (REST), SQL databases, or Excel/CSV data pipelines

 - Exposure to lab environments, scientific instrumentation, or research data workflows

 - Prior experience at a startup or in a fast-paced technical environment




WHY THIS ROLE IS EXCITING

 - Real-world impact: You'll directly accelerate the work of PhD scientists at major companies — what you build makes their research faster and more reproduci

source_jobs.title: Scientific Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in Uncountable Engineering!

 

‍Uncountable is seeking recent graduates interested in a career in data engineering to help manage customers datasets. Our goal is to revolutionize industrial research and development. We’re looking for motivated software engineers who can help to build out tools that will accelerate the development of new chemicals and materials.

 

Your primary responsibility will be to structure and ingest customer data for the Uncountable Web Platform where scientists enter and analyze their experiments. In this role you will (1) manipulate, transform and upload R&D data by writing Python scripts (2) setup ETL pipelines between Uncountable and other data warehouses used by our customers (3) provide new solutions for users to bring in and export their data. This position demands a creative and analytical thinker with cross-functional communication skills, and the ability to analyze large datasets and identify the steps needed to clean, process and upload the data into our platform.

 
 

Requirements

Computer science, data analysis and/or data engineering coursework

Interest in a software engineering career and technology startups

Affinity and familiarity working with data in Excel

Scripting or coding experience in Python to efficiently manipulate large volumes of data

 

Preferred Qualifications

Knowledge of SQL, experience writing queries and working with databases

 

Benefits

Competitive Salary and Equity (ISO Grant)

Health and Dental Insurance

401K with Employer Contribution

17 days PTO annually

 



What's next? Learn more about Interviewing at Uncountable https://www.uncountable.com/interviewing-at-uncountable

 

Learn more about our engineering team: Check out our blog https://medium.com/uncountable-engineering

source_jobs.title: Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
Description

Uncountable is looking for an exceptional account management professional to join our growing team. We’re open to hiring at multiple levels (Account Manager, Enterprise Account Manager, Sr. Enterprise Account Manager, Director of Account Management) and will match level and compensation based on skills and experience.

 

This role is ideal for someone who thrives on building and expanding strategic relationships with large, complex organizations. If you have a proven track record of successful customer engagements and are excited about driving impact in a fast-paced startup environment, we’d love to hear from you.

 

Experience delivering analytics products or enterprise software platforms into large organizations is a major plus. 

 

Key responsibilities

- Own post-sales success for Uncountable’s customers, driving renewals, expansion, and advocacy.

- Lead implementation projects, manage timelines, and keep both internal teams and customer stakeholders aligned.

- Increase customer engagement through regular check-ins, business reviews, and deep knowledge of customer activity.

- Become a trusted advisor by demonstrating ROI, offering strategic guidance, and ensuring customer success.

- Partner with Sales to design and execute expansion strategies for key accounts.

- Collaborate with Implementation and Product teams to identify improvements to the product and customer journey.

- Act as the “voice of the customer” internally, advocating for customer needs and improvements.

 
 
 

Requirements

We are hiring at multiple levels. While exact expectations vary, here’s a rough guide:

Account Manager - Enterprise Account Manager (2–5 years): Prior experience in customer success, account management, or consulting. Skilled in managing customer relationships and project delivery.

Senior Account Manager (5+ years): Track record of independently managing enterprise accounts and influencing executive stakeholders. Strong mentoring skills.

Director (8-10+ years): Deep experience leading strategic accounts, building executive partnerships, and driving long-term account expansion strategies.

 

For all levels:

- Outstanding executive presence and communication skills.

- Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.

- A proactive, entrepreneurial mindset.

- Ability to travel 20–30%.

- Experience working with complex, multi-divisional, multi-geographic customers.

 

Preferred Qualifications

Passion for science, chemistry or artificial intelligence

An aptitude for statistics, mathematics



Salary: 100-180K USD + Commission + Equity

 

Compensation will vary by level and region

source_jobs.title: ML Solution Consultant
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in Uncountable Implementations!




ABOUT UNCOUNTABLE

Science moves slowly — not because researchers aren't brilliant, but because their tools haven't kept up. Uncountable is changing that. We build a unified R&D platform used by the world's leading chemists, material scientists, and biologists to dramatically accelerate how new products are discovered, developed, and brought to market.

Founded by engineers from MIT and Stanford, and trusted by enterprise R&D organizations across chemicals, pharmaceuticals, advanced materials, and food & beverage, Uncountable is on a mission to accelerate industrial R&D by an order of magnitude. We're a high-impact team that moves fast and gives people real ownership from day one.




THE ROLE

Machine learning is only as powerful as the people who can apply it in context — and in industrial R&D, that context is everything. As an ML Solution Consultant at Uncountable, you'll be the person who bridges deep technical expertise in data science and statistical modeling with a genuine understanding of how R&D scientists think, experiment, and make decisions.

You'll work directly with leading R&D organizations to deploy Uncountable's AI capabilities, guiding customers through the full arc of adoption: from understanding their scientific challenges and data structures, to designing optimal experimentation strategies, to ensuring they're extracting real, measurable value from our platform's ML tools.

This is not a back-office data science role. You'll be customer-facing, highly autonomous, and operating at the frontier of applied ML in science — advising PhD-level researchers and R&D directors on how to use AI to accelerate their most important work. You'll also have a direct line to product and engineering, shaping how our platform evolves based on what you see in the field.

If you want to do meaningful applied ML work that reaches the real world — and have the communication skills to bring scientists along with you — this role is for you.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Deploy AI Capabilities with Customers

 - Partner directly with R&D teams at enterprise customers to understand their scientific challenges, data environments, and experimentation workflows

 - Guide customers through onboarding onto Uncountable's ML tools, ensuring data is well-structured and models are configured to reflect their specific scientific context

 - Help customers interpret model outputs, act on recommendations, and build confidence in AI-driven experimentation over time

Serve as a Technical and Scientific Expert

 - Act as a subject-matter expert in statistical modeling, machine learning, and experimental design — advising customers on strategies that maximize the value of their data

 - Translate complex ML concepts into clear, actionable guidance for scientists and R&D leaders who may not have a data science background

 - Troubleshoot modeling challenges, identify data quality issues, and design solutions that make the science work

Drive Cross-Functional Impact

 - Collaborate closely with Uncountable's product and engineering teams, bringing structured customer feedback and real-world usage patterns to inform platform development

 - Identify recurring challenges and opportunities across customer engagements that can be addressed through new features or improved workflows

 - Contribute to internal knowledge-sharing on ML best practices, customer patterns, and domain-specific modeling approaches




REQUIREMENTS

 - ML and data science depth: Strong foundation in machine learning, statistical modeling, or applied statistics — comfortable with experimental design, model evaluation, and working with messy, real-world scientific data

 - Scientific domain fluency: Experience working with R&D data, physical experimentation workflows, or scientific datasets — enough to speak credibly with researchers about their work

 - Communication: Exceptional ability to translate technical concepts into busin

source_jobs.title: Platform Engineer - Generative AI
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in Uncountable Engineering!

 

Description

‍Uncountable is seeking driven software engineers with a focus on Generative AI deployment in software. 

 

Uncountable’s software platform is used by scientists in leading scientific R&D organizations to structure and analyze experimental data. Uncountable’s users are solving critical problems in materials science, chemistry, biotech, and other scientific fields, and our goal is to help them solve those problems faster.

 

Your role will be to bring the full power of ongoing advances in LLMs to bear on real-world problems and friction faced by scientists every day. You’ll be building AI-powered search and visualization tools, next-generation UX that use multimodal LLMs as the UI, intelligent research assistants, and more. This is a unique opportunity to join the front-line effort to accelerate scientific progress using LLMs and generative AI.

 

Primary Responsibility

You will be responsible for:

- Building specific LLM powered features end-to-end, including both LLM-specific tasks (prompting, optimizing latency, tuning/testing behavior) and traditional fullstack development tasks (coding frontends/backends, designing APIs and databases)

- Developing a great in-house LLM stack to serve the evolving needs of our product (API integration, testing, observability, caching, infrastructure for fine-tuning and inference, etc)

 

Uncountable has a strong culture of continuous deployment and fast iteration cycles. You’ll expect to ship your work early and often to Uncountable’s users and iterate based on their feedback. 

 

Many of the features you build will represent new and unprecedented ways to use software, so you’ll wear various hats as a designer, researcher, engineer, product manager, and whatever else is needed to create an amazing product. This is a great role for someone who is looking to grow their skills and perspective quickly, or who wants to found their own company in the future.

 

Salary Range: $120K-$160K + Equity



Requirements

 - ‍‍2 years of development experience

 - Strong interest in Generative AI

 - Solid computer science and software engineering fundamentals



Preferred Qualifications

 - ‍‍B.S. in computer science

 - Experience shipping software incorporating Generative AI to users (e.g. started an LLM startup or shipped a side project with real usage)

 - Familiarity with fullstack web development



Our Current Stack

 - Backend: Flask (Python), Postgres

 - Frontend: React (Typescript), Redux, Sass



Benefits

 - Competitive Salary and Equity

 - Health and Dental Insurance

 - 401K with Employer Contribution



What's next? Learn more about Interviewing at Uncountable https://www.uncountable.com/interviewing-at-uncountable

 

Learn more about our engineering team: Check out our blog https://medium.com/uncountable-engineering

source_jobs.title: Partner Implementation Manager
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in Uncountable Implementations!




ABOUT UNCOUNTABLE

Science moves slowly — not because researchers aren't brilliant, but because their tools haven't kept up. Uncountable is changing that. We build a unified R&D platform used by the world's leading chemists, material scientists, and biologists to dramatically accelerate how new products are discovered, developed, and brought to market.

Founded by engineers from MIT and Stanford, and trusted by enterprise R&D organizations across chemicals, pharmaceuticals, advanced materials, and food & beverage, Uncountable is on a mission to accelerate industrial R&D by an order of magnitude. We're a high-impact team that moves fast and gives people real ownership from day one.




THE ROLE

Uncountable's partner network is a critical lever for global growth — but a partner-led implementation is only as good as the expertise behind it. As a Partner Delivery Manager, you are the person who ensures that every implementation delivered through a certified partner meets the same standard of scientific rigor, data quality, and customer outcome that Uncountable's internal team is known for.

This role is closely related to our Scientific Implementation Manager function, with an added dimension: you're not just running implementations yourself — you're raising the capability of an entire partner ecosystem to do so. That means serving as the resident expert partners turn to when configurations get complex, building the training and enablement infrastructure that sets them up for success, and maintaining a quality bar that protects Uncountable's reputation and customer relationships at scale.

You'll work directly with both partner teams and end customers, requiring the same scientific fluency, platform depth, and communication skills as our implementation managers — plus the ability to teach, coach, and build systems that work without you in the room.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Oversee Partner-Led Implementations

 - Own quality assurance across all partner-delivered implementations, serving as the final authority on whether configurations meet Uncountable's standards before go-live

 - Stay closely involved in active partner implementations — reviewing data structures, attending key customer milestones, and stepping in to course-correct when needed

 - Maintain a clear picture of implementation health across the partner portfolio, identifying risks early and driving resolution

Serve as the Expert Resource for Partners

 - Act as the primary point of escalation for certified delivery partners when they encounter complex scientific data environments, edge-case configurations, or ambiguous customer requirements

 - Guide partners through challenging implementations in real time — answering technical and scientific questions, reviewing proposed configurations, and helping them think through trade-offs

 - Build deep, trusted relationships with partner teams so they feel supported and confident deploying Uncountable without hand-holding on every engagement

Design and Configure Complex Scientific Workflows

 - Directly lead or co-lead implementations for strategic accounts, ensuring the platform is configured in a way that faithfully captures each customer's experimental paradigm

 - Map intricate scientific data structures — multi-variable formulations, hierarchical sample relationships, linked test methods — onto Uncountable's data model

 - Work through ambiguous, real-world data environments (legacy spreadsheets, idiosyncratic naming conventions, non-standard workflows) and design configurations that are both scientifically accurate and practically usable

 - Develop and document reusable configuration patterns across domains (coatings, polymers, pharmaceuticals, food science, etc.) that can be distributed across the partner network

Build Partner Training and Enablement

 - Design and own Uncountable's partner enablement program — including onboarding curriculum, certificati

source_jobs.title: Head of PLM Growth
source_jobs.description:
ROLE OVERVIEW

We are seeking a high-agency, full-lifecycle Head of PLM Growth to spearhead the commercialization and technical deployment of our Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) solution.

In this role, you will own the revenue pipeline for the PLM product line—acting to discover, negotiate, and close enterprise deals.

This is a "full-stack" entrepreneurial role demanding exceptional enterprise sales acumen.




LOCATION

Preference for New York on London. Remote in the U.S. or UK is also possible.




COMPENSATION

 - Base Salary: $120,000 - $160,000

 - OTE: $200,000 - $250,000

 - Incentive Compensation: Fraction of PLM revenue generated

 - Equity: Options in Uncountable Inc.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Full-Cycle Sales Ownership: Manage the enterprise sales funnel from qualified lead to execution of master services agreements (MSAs) and statements of work (SOWs).

 - Technical Discovery & Value Realization: Map complex R&D, product development, and quality management workflows to Uncountable’s PLM capabilities, demonstrating ROI to VP and C-level stakeholders.

 - Commercial Negotiation: Navigate complex enterprise procurement, legal, and IT security reviews to successfully close six-figure ARR contracts.

 - Product Feedback Loop: Act as the strategic bridge between the market and our core engineering team, translating customer requirements and sales friction points into high-priority product roadmap items.




QUALIFICATIONS & TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS



Domain Expertise: Minimum 2+ years of experience within the PLM space, either selling, implementing, or heavily utilizing these systems within relevant enterprise environments (e.g., chemicals, materials, CPG, or life sciences).

Proven Commercial Track Record: Experience managing complex B2B enterprise SaaS sales cycles ($100k+ ARR) with a demonstrable history of meeting or exceeding revenue targets.

High Agency: Comfortable operating autonomously in an ambiguous environment; willing to pivot rapidly between high-level strategic negotiations and granular, tactical system configuration.

source_jobs.title: Scientific Implementation Manager
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in Uncountable Implementations!




ABOUT UNCOUNTABLE

Science moves slowly — not because researchers aren't brilliant, but because their tools haven't kept up. Uncountable is changing that. We build a unified R&D platform used by the world's leading chemists, material scientists, and biologists to dramatically accelerate how new products are discovered, developed, and brought to market.

Founded by engineers from MIT and Stanford, and trusted by enterprise R&D organizations across chemicals, pharmaceuticals, advanced materials, and food & beverage, Uncountable is on a mission to accelerate industrial R&D by an order of magnitude. We're a high-impact team that moves fast and gives people real ownership from day one.




THE ROLE

This is not a typical customer success role. As a Scientific Implementation Manager, you are the person who bridges the gap between a scientist's world and Uncountable's platform — and that requires something most software companies can't offer: someone who genuinely understands both.

You'll work directly with R&D leaders, bench scientists, and technical teams at some of the world's most innovative industrial companies, helping them translate years of experimental knowledge into a structured, powerful data environment. You'll configure the platform to reflect how their science actually works, identify where their data lives and how to bring it together, and guide them through a transformation in how they manage and leverage their R&D.

At the same time, you'll be a critical voice internally — your customer-facing experience will directly shape product development, ensuring we keep building the most useful R&D platform in the industry.

This is a fast-paced, project-driven role that sits at the intersection of science, business, and software. If you want to use your scientific background to do something genuinely impactful beyond the bench, this is it.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Lead Scientific Implementations

 - Serve as the primary scientific point of contact for new customers through the full onboarding journey

 - Translate a customer's experimental workflows, data structures, and scientific domain into a tailored Uncountable configuration

 - Restructure and contextualize customer data so it becomes immediately useful, searchable, and actionable in the platform

Drive Customer Engagement

 - Lead discovery calls, demos, and working sessions with R&D managers, scientists, and technical stakeholders

 - Build trusted relationships by demonstrating genuine scientific fluency — you'll earn credibility because you understand their work, not just their software

 - Set project timelines, manage implementation milestones, and ensure customers hit their go-live goals

Design and Configure Complex Scientific Workflows

 - Architect end-to-end platform configurations that faithfully capture a customer's experimental paradigm — from how they define formulations and design experiments, to how they record measurements and track results

 - Map intricate scientific data structures (multi-variable formulations, hierarchical sample relationships, linked test methods) onto Uncountable's data model, making principled decisions about how best to represent each customer's science

 - Work through ambiguous, edge-case data environments — customers with years of legacy spreadsheets, idiosyncratic naming conventions, and non-standard workflows — and design configurations that are both scientifically accurate and practically usable

 - Iterate on platform configurations in close collaboration with customers, running working sessions where you test, refine, and validate that the system reflects how their team actually thinks about their research

 - Develop and document reusable configuration patterns across domains (coatings, polymers, pharmaceuticals, food science, etc.) that can accelerate future implementations and inform product development

Shape the Product

 - Act as the voice of the custome

source_jobs.title: Validation Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT UNCOUNTABLE

Uncountable builds software that helps R&D teams manage data, workflows, and experimentation at scale. Our platform is used by scientific and engineering teams across regulated and non-regulated industries, and quality, reliability, and compliance are core to how we build and ship product.




ROLE OVERVIEW

The Validation Engineer is responsible for ensuring that Uncountable’s software systems meet internal quality standards and applicable regulatory requirements. This role partners closely with Engineering, Product, and Quality to design, execute, and maintain validation activities throughout the software development lifecycle.

Currently, we do not sponsor employment visas for this position. Applicants must be legally authorized to work in the US without current or future sponsorship.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Plan, execute, and maintain software validation activities in accordance with company procedures and applicable regulations (e.g., FedRamp, GxP, FDA, ISO, as applicable).

 - Author and maintain validation documentation, including validation plans, risk assessments, test protocols, test reports, and traceability matrices.

 - Review system requirements, design specifications, and user stories to ensure they are testable and appropriately controlled.

 - Execute and/or review functional, integration, regression, and user acceptance testing.

 - Support change control activities by assessing validation impact and updating documentation as needed.

 - Collaborate with Engineering and Product to identify risks early and ensure quality is built into the development process.

 - Participate in audits and inspections by preparing documentation and supporting responses.

 - Continuously improve validation processes, templates, and tools to increase efficiency and scalability.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Life Sciences, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).

 - Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple validation activities in parallel.

 - Clear written and verbal communication skills.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience validating SaaS or cloud-based applications.

 - Familiarity with GxP, 21 CFR Part 11, ISO 9001, or similar regulatory frameworks.

 - Experience working with cross-functional teams in a fast-paced product environment.

 - Prior experience supporting customer or internal audits.

source_jobs.title: Content Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Uncountable:

Uncountable is an innovative B2B SaaS company providing an AI-driven Laboratory Informatics platform for R&D organizations. Uncountable is transforming how R&D enterprises operate—replacing siloed and fragmented legacy data systems and Excel spreadsheets in the lab with a unified software designed to accelerate scientific product development and innovation. We help scientists maximize utilization of their experimental data, automate the capture of high-quality, structured data, and enable them to make faster, more intelligent data-driven decisions. At our core, we are a laboratory informatics company...but we go further. 

 

Uncountable is also a leader in applying AI to scientific workflows, helping R&D teams not only manage data but also contextualize and learn from it, thereby optimizing formulations, surfacing insights, and bringing market-leading products to market faster and more effectively. We partner with the world’s leading companies across various industries, including advanced materials, chemicals, pharmaceuticals, biotechnology, food and beverage, cosmetics, and many more, to build the lab of the future.

 

Description:

Uncountable seeks a results-oriented Content Marketing Manager to join our small yet powerful marketing team. This role requires someone 100% committed to working in a fast-paced, young startup environment, with motivation and drive to proactively lead and/or identify new and/or existing content marketing efforts. This person must quickly understand, develop, and engage large organizations through diverse multi-channel content marketing efforts. As a small marketing team, this role will be pivotal in the strategic development and coordination, creation, management, and optimization of all content marketing activities. The ideal candidate will possess a strong self-starter mentality and the ability to thrive and succeed within a highly technical B2B SaaS organization.

 

The Content Marketing Manager will strategize, create, manage, promote, and optimize engaging content across multiple channels and mediums, including the blog, social media, email marketing campaigns, newsletters, webinars, case studies, white papers, sales collateral, and other relevant platforms. This candidate must possess strong written and verbal communication skills, as well as storytelling abilities, with the capacity to tailor messaging and narratives to various target audiences and industries. 



Key Responsibilities:

 - Collaborate with marketing, product, and sales teams to develop engaging, relevant content that communicates our product's value to our target audience.

 - Manage our editorial calendar (maintaining consistent cadence, brand tone, messaging, content, SEO objectives, etc.), ensuring all content is published, promoted, and shared/distributed with any key stakeholders on time.

 - Analyze performance data to measure content marketing strategies' effectiveness and inform future content creation.

 - Identify, create, and optimize content from social media, downloadable content, case studies, presentation decks, event collateral, webinar materials, etc.

 - Analyze and optimize our on-page and off-page SEO efforts (e.g., website pages, blog, backlinking, etc.) 

 - Maintain a strategic cadence of organic content via the blog and social media posting across all channels.

 - Develop and manage the creation of materials supporting webinars and thought-leadership engagements – including topic development and creation, presentation deliverables, abstract, digital and/or physical assets, pre and post-event email or partnership promotional efforts, etc.

 - Lead the creation of sales enablement materials, such as case studies, white papers, presentation decks, one-pagers, and product sheets, to support sales team efforts.

 - Conduct market and competitor research to ensure our content remains compelling and relevant in the B2B SaaS industry.

 - Utilize SEO best practices to increase the vi

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager, Implementations
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

 

Uncountable is looking for an Engineering Manager to lead our Implementations Software Engineering team. In this role, you’ll collaborate directly with both internal engineers and external stakeholders at some of the world’s leading materials and chemical companies. You will guide your team in building scientific applications and integrating enterprise systems with Uncountable’s platform, helping customers transform how R&D gets done. You’ll be at the intersection of leadership, technical architecture, and customer engagement—contributing to Uncountable’s mission of accelerating innovation across industries like chemicals, coatings, pharma, and more.

 
 

Why Uncountable?

 

Uncountable was founded by a team of Stanford and MIT engineers who believed that R&D teams deserved better software. Today, we’re building a platform that helps customers like Sika, Clariant and Mondelez drive innovation faster than ever before.Join us if you’re passionate about impactful software, world-class science, and empowering teams to push the boundaries of innovation.

source_jobs.title: Partner Manager – US
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

We are seeking a Partner Manager to join our growing team. In this role, you will work alongside the Partnerships Director to support and enable Uncountable’s US-based strategic partners, ensuring they are well-connected to our sales process and positioned for success. You will help strengthen relationships with industry leaders such as Zifo, Astrix, Accenture, Deloitte, AWS, Microsoft, and others in the US market — enabling smooth collaboration and driving impactful joint initiatives.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone who enjoys working in a collaborative role that combines partner enablement, sales support, and coordination. You will report directly to our Director of Partnerships and have a clear career growth path — including the potential to step into the Partnerships Director role as we expand.



Responsibilities

• Support the Partnerships Director in managing and developing relationships with US-based strategic partners.

• Coordinate with the sales team to connect partners to relevant opportunities and assist in joint go-to-market activities.

• Assist in onboarding new US partners, ensuring they understand Uncountable’s solutions, positioning, and sales process.

• Support partner enablement by organizing training sessions, sharing best practices, and facilitating communication between partner teams and Uncountable’s internal teams.

• Collaborate with Marketing to develop co-branded materials, joint case studies, and event participation strategies.

• Help maintain engagement with US-based SIs, Global Consultancies, and Implementation & Managed Service Providers to align on customer needs and market trends.

• Track partner activity, pipeline, and feedback to inform strategy and improve outcomes.

• Travel up to 25% to join partner meetings, attend events, and support joint sales efforts in the US.



Qualifications

• 5+ years of experience in partner management, channel sales, alliances, customer success, or services — ideally in enterprise SaaS or R&D software.

• Familiarity with US-based SIs, Global Consultancies, and technology partner ecosystems.

• Experience supporting relationships with large strategic partners (e.g., Zifo, Astrix, Wega, AWS, Microsoft, Deloitte, Accenture).

• Strong organizational, communication, and coordination skills.

• Comfortable working in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

• Willingness to travel up to 25% domestically, with occasional international travel if required.



Why Join Us

• Work closely with the Partnerships Director and sales leadership team.

• Clear career progression, including the potential to advance into the Partnerships Director role.

• Build exposure to partnerships with some of the biggest names in tech and consulting in the US.

• Join a collaborative, innovative, and supportive team culture.

source_jobs.title: Scientific Implementation Manager - APAC
source_jobs.description:
Uncountable is seeking a scientific implementation manager to support our Customers in APAC/China with a breadth of scientific experience to bring into a career in business development. The scientific implementations role at Uncountable leverages your scientific understanding and problem solving skills to bring both the scientist and their science into the Uncountable ecosystem.

 

In this role you will (1) lead scientific implementation of the Uncountable platform (2) restructure and contextualize customer data and (3) use real-world customer experience to drive product development so we can build the best possible data structuring tool for R&D. It is fast paced, project oriented, customer and engineering facing role. This position demands a sophisticated breadth of scientific knowledge, impeccable communication skills, and the ability to empathize both emotionally, scientifically, and organizationally with a variety of users spanning the R&D landscape.

 

Within 30 days you will take ownership of experimental understanding and ingestion of onboarded customers. You will work with both the customer success and sales team to expertly configure data templates and scientific workflows within the platform to the end-user.

 

Within 60 days you will begin to lead meetings and demos with new customers, running scientific due diligence, acquiring new data, and running through iterations of the platform configuration with the customer.

 

Within 90 days you will take ownership of the implementation process for a new and potential customers. You will lead demos, orchestrate meetings, set timelines, and advocate for new product development to meet the unique needs of our most exciting customers. 

 

Salary: 90-120K USD Per Year, in addition to performance bonus and equity

 



Relevant Experience, Skills, and Preferences

 - Requirements: Fluent in English and Mandarin

 - Master's degree or 2+ years experience in some scientific field (chemistry, biology, materials science, chemical engineering, or related fields)

 - Affinity and familiarity working with data in Excel (Python or other coding skills a plus)

 - Comfort distilling and effectively communicating advanced technical concepts

 - Willingness and ability to travel up 20% of the time

source_jobs.title: LLM Applications Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in Uncountable Engineering!



Description

Uncountable is seeking experienced engineers to lead the transformation of our platform into an AI-first R&D ecosystem. We are building the next generation of tools that empower scientists at Fortune 500 companies to accelerate discovery through Generative AI.

As an LLM Applications Engineer, you will be the architect of our LLM infrastructure. You won't just be building interfaces; you will be designing the retrieval systems, agentic workflows, and data pipelines that bridge the gap between complex experimental data and actionable AI insights. Your work will directly modernize how the world’s leading researchers interact with their data.



Primary Responsibility

Your primary responsibility is to design, build, and scale the LLM infrastructure and user-facing AI features of the Uncountable Web Platform. This is a hybrid role requiring a deep understanding of full-stack development and the unique challenges of deploying LLMs in production.

 - Infrastructure: Implement and optimize RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipelines, vector databases, and prompt management systems.

 - Feature Development: Build intuitive, AI-driven UI components that allow scientists to query, analyze, and visualize complex experimental datasets.

 - Integration: Connect LLM capabilities with our core SQL databases and data analysis engines to ensure high-fidelity, grounded AI responses.



Salary Range: $130K–$175K + Equity



Requirements

 - 2+ years of software development experience with a focus on full-stack web applications.

 - Proven experience with LLM orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, or Haystack).

 - Proficiency in Python and JavaScript/TypeScript: Ability to work across the AI backend and the React frontend.

 - Solid Engineering Fundamentals: Experience with API design, asynchronous processing, and performance optimization.



Preferred Qualifications

 - B.S. in Computer Science or a related technical field.

 - Experience with LLM Evaluation: Knowledge of how to measure and mitigate "hallucinations" in a scientific/technical context.

 - Familiarity with SQL: Specifically optimizing queries that serve as the context for LLM prompts.

 - Product Mindset: A passion for creating "LLM-first" user experiences that feel seamless rather than bolted-on.



Our Current Stack

 - Backend: Flask (Python), Postgres

 - Frontend: React (Typescript), Redux, Sass



Benefits

 - Competitive Salary and Equity

 - Health and Dental Insurance

 - 401K with Employer Contribution



What's next? Learn more about Interviewing at Uncountable https://www.uncountable.com/interviewing-at-uncountable



Learn more about our engineering team: Check out our blog https://medium.com/uncountable-engineering

source_jobs.title: Scientific Implementation Associate
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in Uncountable Implementations!




SCIENTIFIC IMPLEMENTATION ASSOCIATE




ABOUT UNCOUNTABLE

Science moves slowly — not because researchers aren't brilliant, but because their tools haven't kept up. Uncountable is changing that. We build a unified R&D platform used by the world's leading chemists, material scientists, and biologists to dramatically accelerate how new products are discovered, developed, and brought to market.

Founded by engineers from MIT and Stanford, and trusted by enterprise R&D organizations across chemicals, pharmaceuticals, advanced materials, and food & beverage, Uncountable is on a mission to accelerate industrial R&D by an order of magnitude. We're a high-impact team that moves fast and gives people real ownership from day one.




THE ROLE

This role is built for a scientist who is excited about what happens when good data infrastructure meets great research — and who wants to be on the front lines of making that happen.

As a Scientific Implementation Associate, you'll work directly with R&D teams at some of the world's leading industrial companies, helping them bring their science into the Uncountable platform. That means understanding how they design experiments, what data they generate, and how they think about their workflows — then translating all of that into a platform configuration that actually reflects their science.

It's hands-on, technically engaging work. You'll spend time restructuring and contextualizing real customer datasets, designing data models that capture complex experimental relationships, and iterating on configurations in close collaboration with scientists and engineers. Over time, you'll also take on increasing ownership of full customer implementations — from first meeting through go-live.

This is an ideal entry point for someone with a scientific background who wants to build a career at the intersection of science, software, and customer impact — without leaving their domain expertise behind.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Support Scientific Implementations

 - Work alongside senior implementation team members to onboard new customers and support existing ones with new implementation initiatives

 - Learn and apply Uncountable's implementation methodology to configure the platform for individual customers' experimental workflows and data environments

 - Take on increasing ownership of implementations over time, eventually leading your own from end to end

Design and Configure Complex Scientific Workflows

 - Translate customers' experimental paradigms — how they define formulations, design experiments, record measurements, and track results — into accurate, well-structured platform configurations

 - Map scientific data structures (multi-variable formulations, hierarchical sample relationships, linked test methods) onto Uncountable's data model, making principled decisions about how to best represent each customer's science

 - Work through ambiguous, real-world data environments — customers with years of legacy spreadsheets, idiosyncratic naming conventions, and non-standard workflows — and design configurations that are both scientifically accurate and practically usable

 - Iterate on platform configurations in working sessions with customers, testing and refining until the system genuinely reflects how their team thinks about their research

Engage Directly with Customers

 - Join and eventually lead meetings with R&D scientists, data managers, and technical stakeholders

 - Build credibility through scientific fluency — understanding customers' domains well enough to ask the right questions and propose the right solutions

 - Communicate clearly and empathetically across a wide range of audiences, from bench scientists to R&D directors

Contribute to Team Knowledge

 - Help document reusable configuration patterns across domains (coatings, polymers, pharma, food science, etc.) that accelerate future implementations

 - Share observations from

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
Description:

 

The Uncountable Sales team is looking to add a superstar Account Executive to the team with experience in software sales and B2B sales. The ideal candidate thrives in a start-up environment where they can not only make a tremendous impact on the company, but also help shape the sales strategy. This individual will be focused on continuing to tackle the materials and chemicals space where we have a proven track record and now focused on scaling.

 

Key responsibilities:

 

- Establish and develop a strategy for identifying and closing net new sales opportunities.

- Manage the entire sales cycle from prospecting to closing new business. 

- Deliver sales pitches and demos to C-level executives and VPs both over the phone and in-person (eg. become a demo expert!)

- Build proposals and handle complex negotiations

- Leverage sales tools: SFDC, Zoominfo, Linkedin Sales Nav, and Outreach

- Attend customer application conferences around the country to learn about the customer needs and build connections from line scientists to executive decision makers

 

What we are looking for:

 

- Professional experience selling into Enterprise sized organizations 

- Strong experience in prospecting and self-generating pipeline

- Proven experience in developing champions and managing multiple stakeholders in a complex sales cycle 

- Curiosity - you will be working across many different end customer applications and want to feel comfortable engaging with a broad range of technical people.

- Availability to travel 25-50% of the time

- Availability to take morning calls with the east coast and Europe (7am PST!)

 

Preferred Qualifications:

 

- Bachelor’s Degree

- Past experience working in an early stage start-up environment

- Knowledge of chemistry, materials science, or biology



Salary: 120-160K Base, 240-320K OTE + Equity

source_jobs.title: Solutions Engineer

Travel = 25-50%

source_jobs.title: Scientific Support Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in Uncountable Implementations!




SCIENTIFIC SUPPORT SPECIALIST




ABOUT UNCOUNTABLE

Science moves slowly — not because researchers aren't brilliant, but because their tools haven't kept up. Uncountable is changing that. We build a unified R&D platform used by the world's leading chemists, material scientists, and biologists to dramatically accelerate how new products are discovered, developed, and brought to market.

Founded by engineers from MIT and Stanford, and trusted by enterprise R&D organizations across chemicals, pharmaceuticals, advanced materials, and food & beverage, Uncountable is on a mission to accelerate industrial R&D by an order of magnitude. We're a high-impact team that moves fast and gives people real ownership from day one.




THE ROLE

This role is built for a scientist who is as energized by solving a customer's problem at 9am as they are by closing out the last ticket of the day — and who takes genuine pride in being the person customers can count on.

As a Scientific Support Specialist, you will own steady-state support for Uncountable's SMB customers and a select group of designated mid-market accounts. You are the first line of contact when something isn't working, when a customer has a question, or when a new need surfaces — and you're responsible for making sure every one of those moments is handled with speed, care, and follow-through.

While programming skills are not needed for this role , this is technically demanding, customer-facing work. You'll triage and resolve inbound support tickets, investigate platform behavior, coordinate with the Scientific Implementations team on continuity across accounts, and escalate clearly to engineering when needed. You'll also play a proactive role in identifying patterns, improving processes, and making sure issues get fixed — not just worked around.

This is a fully remote position. Depending on your regional location, you will work during standard US or EU business hours to provide consistent, real-time coverage for your assigned customer accounts. Strong communication habits and the ability to operate independently are essential.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Own Steady-State Customer Support

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for a portfolio of SMB and designated mid-market accounts, handling inbound questions, issues, and requests end-to-end

 - Triage, investigate, and resolve support tickets with urgency and precision

 - Communicate with customers clearly, empathetically, and proactively — always putting their success first and never leaving an issue in an undefined state

Troubleshoot and Escalate Effectively

 - Investigate platform behavior, configuration issues, and data problems across a range of customer environments

 - Identify when an issue requires engineering involvement and draft clear, actionable escalation summaries that enable fast resolution

 - Follow escalated issues through to closure, maintaining customer-facing transparency throughout

Collaborate with the SI and Engineering Teams

 - Work closely with Scientific Implementation Associates and Managers to ensure smooth continuity between active implementation engagements and steady-state support

 - Surface recurring issues, systemic bugs, and friction points to internal product and engineering teams — advocating for fixes rather than workarounds

 - Contribute to support tooling and workflow improvements, including standardized ticket structures, phase workflows, and Slack-based notification integrations

Be Proactive, Not Reactive

 - Monitor account health across your portfolio and flag early warning signs before they become escalations

 - Anticipate the follow-up question, not just the one that was asked

 - Document ticket history, resolution steps, and account-specific context meticulously — so knowledge is shared, not siloed




YOUR FIRST 180 DAYS

Month 1: Develop deep fluency in Uncountable's platform and support workflows. Begin triaging

notes: High volume of engineering and technical roles, with a focus on AI and data science. Strong presence of sales and business development roles, but engineering core remains dominant.
